title: Build a correct training loop
description: Structure the epoch loop so gradients are zeroed, loss is on the right data, and validation runs without gradient tracking.
body: Steps: 1. For each epoch, set the model to train mode and iterate batches. 2. Zero gradients, forward, compute loss, backward, optimizer step — in that order. 3. Track training loss as a running average, not the last batch. 4. After the epoch, switch to eval mode and run validation under no-grad, computing the same loss/metrics on the validation split. 5. Log both curves and checkpoint on best validation. Rollback point: before changing the optimizer or learning-rate schedule. Validation: training loss decreases, the missing zero-grad or eval-mode bug would show as exploding loss or train/val leakage; a loop without no-grad validation silently inflates memory and metrics.
